action.hypothesis: Final reconstruction stays x_hat = sum_{r=1..R} beta_r(x) u_r + sum_{i in S_{e(x-t(x))}(x-t(x))} alpha_{e,i}(x) b_{e,i}, with t(x) = sum_{r=1..R} beta_r(x) u_r. This exports to the LUTurbo/Lottable path as a static trunk table lookup plus a single routed static expert sublibrary lookup plus a finite weighted sum, so `lowrank_expert_topk` is extended-compatible. Compatibility screen before proposing this round: `expert_topk` is directly compatible; `lowrank_expert_topk`, `lowrank_expert_residual`, and `two_stage_residual_expert` are extended-compatible; batch-coupled families such as `batch_topk` and `adaptive_budget_topk` are incompatible and must stay off the active frontier. Relative to r60, changing only `TRUNK_RANK` from 64 to 32 tests the missing mid-trunk point in the low-cost band: if the routed-only improvement from r59 to r60 was not all-or-nothing, this should recover much of r60's FVU while moving left from the current lowrank+expert anchor and potentially create a new compatible Pareto segment between `expert_topk` and `lowrank_expert_residual`.
action.summary: Use Round 70 for a low-cost structural probe in `lowrank_expert_topk`: start from r60 and only reduce `TRUNK_RANK` to 32. The goal is to map whether shared low-rank trunk width can produce a new compatible Pareto point in the 0.3x-0.5x band, instead of continuing plain-selector K sweeps or more local cleanup on `lowrank_expert_residual`.
action.self_review: This satisfies the single-variable param-only rule and stays within an already implemented compatible family. It directly answers a higher-value structural question than another `expert_topk` K move, and it avoids spending Round 70 on further local polishing of the current `lowrank_expert_residual` mainline after r66 already showed diminishing returns from more trunk compression there.
notes_to_memory: Round 70 targets the under-mapped low-to-mid-cost region with a structural width probe, not another plain-K sweep.
`lowrank_expert_topk` now has informative anchors at `TRUNK_RANK=16` and `64` under `K=128, NUM_EXPERTS=32`; `TRUNK_RANK=32` is the clean missing midpoint for the same compatible family.
Keep incompatible batch-coupled families excluded from frontier accounting; active frontier decisions should continue to use only direct-compatible and extended-compatible families.
next_hypotheses: If `TRUNK_RANK=32` keeps most of r60 quality at materially lower total_cost, next do a matched `ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_expert_residual` switch from this point to measure whether residual cleanup is still worth paying for at the same trunk width.

If `TRUNK_RANK=32` falls back close to the routed-only `expert_topk` band, treat that as evidence that `lowrank_expert_topk` has a sharp trunk-width floor and shift low-cost budget back to `lowrank_expert_residual` or pure `expert_topk` rather than more MoE-capacity expansion.
If throughput or convergence looks anomalous rather than simply worse, use a follow-up midpoint such as `TRUNK_RANK=48` before drawing a structural conclusion.

The goal is to map whether shared low-rank trunk width can produce a new compatible Pareto point in the 0.3x-0.5x band, instead of continuing plain-selector K sweeps or more local cleanup on `lowrank_expert_residual`.

It directly answers a higher-value structural question than another `expert_topk` K move, and it avoids spending Round 70 on further local polishing of the current `lowrank_expert_residual` mainline after r66 already showed diminishing returns from more trunk compression there.
